Home Docs Pricing Login

Example Article

By Test Author

February 1, 2026

This article exists to validate the static HTML extraction path. It contains enough prose to pass the default quality gates, and it places the meaningful content in a semantic article container rather than in a page shell. The paragraphs are deliberately long enough to exercise the density-based scorer and the boilerplate pruning logic.

The second paragraph explains why the tool should stay on HTTP when the body content is complete. It also verifies that navigation, account prompts, and global chrome are not promoted into the Markdown output. The renderer should keep headings, prose, code, tables, and links while removing repeated interface fragments.

Code sample

def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"hello {name}"

Comparison

Below is a simple table and a relative link. The output should preserve the table structure and rewrite the link against the canonical page URL so downstream consumers receive a stable absolute target.

ModeCost
HTTPLow
BrowserHigh

Read the follow-up article for more detail.

Tags: scraping, markdown, observability
